from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of triples about airports (or related entities) into a
    single fluent sentence.  Predicates are mapped to short English phrases;
    unknown predicates fall back to a generic “predicate object” wording.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Helper to turn a predicate + object into a readable phrase
    def phrase(pred: str, obj: str) -> str:
        mapping = {
            "cityServed": "serves the city of {obj}",
            "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": "has an elevation of {obj} metres above sea level",
            "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInFeet": "has an elevation of {obj} feet above sea level",
            "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInMetres": "has an elevation of {obj} metres above sea level",
            "location": "is located in {obj}",
            "operatingOrganisation": "is operated by {obj}",
            "runwayLength": "has a runway length of {obj} metres",
            "runwayName": "has a runway named {obj}",
            "country": "is in {obj}",
            "runwaySurfaceType": "has runway surface type {obj}",
            "1stRunwaySurfaceType": "has first runway surface type {obj}",
            "2ndRunwaySurfaceType": "has second runway surface type {obj}",
            "3rdRunwaySurfaceType": "has third runway surface type {obj}",
            "4thRunwaySurfaceType": "has fourth runway surface type {obj}",
            "5thRunwaySurfaceType": "has fifth runway surface type {obj}",
            "countySeat": "has county seat {obj}",
            "chief": "is led by chief {obj}",
            "foundedBy": "was founded by {obj}",
            "postalCode": "has postal code {obj}",
            "areaCode": "has area code {obj}",
            "foundingYear": "was founded in {obj}",
            "ceremonialCounty": "is in the ceremonial county {obj}",
            "administrativeArrondissement": "is in the administrative arrondissement {obj}",
            "isPartOf": "is part of {obj}",
            "1stRunwayLengthFeet": "has a first runway length of {obj} feet",
            "1stRunwayLengthMetre": "has a first runway length of {obj} metres",
            "1stRunwayNumber": "has a first runway number {obj}",
            "1stRunwaySurfaceType": "has a first runway surface type of {obj}",
            "2ndRunwaySurfaceType": "has a second runway surface type of {obj}",
            "3rdRunwayLengthFeet": "has a third runway length of {obj} feet",
            "3rdRunwaySurfaceType": "has a third runway surface type of {obj}",
            "4thRunwayLengthFeet": "has a fourth runway length of {obj} feet",
            "4thRunwaySurfaceType": "has a fourth runway surface type of {obj}",
            "5thRunwayNumber": "has a fifth runway number {obj}",
            "5thRunwaySurfaceType": "has a fifth runway surface type of {obj}",
            "icaoLocationIdentifier": "has ICAO identifier {obj}",
            "locationIdentifier": "has location identifier {obj}",
            "iataLocationIdentifier": "has IATA identifier {obj}",
            "nativeName": "is also known as {obj}",
            "owner": "is owned by {obj}",
            "largestCity": "has the largest city {obj}",
            "capital": "has capital {obj}",
            "language": "has language {obj}",
            "officialLanguage": "has official language {obj}",
            "leader": "is led by {obj}",
            "leaderParty": "belongs to the party {obj}",
            "leaderTitle": "has leader title {obj}",
            "mayor": "has mayor {obj}",
            "regionServed": "serves the region {obj}",
            "hubAirport": "uses {obj} as a hub airport",
            "aircraftHelicopter": "operates helicopter {obj}",
            "transportAircraft": "operates transport aircraft {obj}",
            "aircraftFighter": "operates fighter aircraft {obj}",
            "attackAircraft": "operates attack aircraft {obj}",
            "battle": "has participated in {obj}",
            "currency": "uses the currency {obj}",
            "headquarter": "has its headquarter at {obj}",
            "jurisdiction": "lies within the jurisdiction of {obj}",
            "demonym": "has demonym {obj}",
            "postalCode": "has postal code {obj}",
            "areaCode": "has area code {obj}",
            "foundingYear": "was founded in {obj}",
            "foundedBy": "was founded by {obj}",
            "ceremonialCounty": "is in the ceremonial county {obj}",
            "administrativeArrondissement": "is in the administrative arrondissement {obj}",
            "class": "belongs to class {obj}",
            "division": "belongs to division {obj}",
            "order": "belongs to order {obj}",
        }
        tmpl = mapping.get(pred, f"{pred.replace('_', ' ')} {{obj}}")
        return tmpl.format(obj=obj)

    # Build clauses with better sentence structure
    main_subject = triples[0].subject
    clauses = []
    used_indices = set()

    # Build a mapping of object -> triples where it's the subject (for chaining)
    object_to_triples = {}
    for i, t in enumerate(triples):
        if t.object not in object_to_triples:
            object_to_triples[t.object] = []
        object_to_triples[t.object].append((i, t))

    # Group triples by subject for better organization
    triples_by_subject = {}
    for i, t in enumerate(triples):
        if t.subject not in triples_by_subject:
            triples_by_subject[t.subject] = []
        triples_by_subject[t.subject].append((i, t))

    # Build connected chains by following entity relationships
    # First handle main subject triples
    main_clauses = []
    for i, t in enumerate(triples):
        if t.subject == main_subject:
            main_clauses.append(phrase(t.predicate, t.object))
            used_indices.add(i)

    # Then find connected triples through location/country relationships
    connected_clauses = []
    remaining_triples = [(i, t) for i, t in enumerate(triples) if i not in used_indices]

    # Try to build chains where object of one triple is subject of another
    for i, t in remaining_triples:
        if i in used_indices:
            continue
        # Check if this object is subject of another triple (for chaining)
        if t.object in object_to_triples and len(object_to_triples[t.object]) > 0:
            # This object has outgoing triples - chain them
            for j, sub_t in object_to_triples[t.object]:
                if j not in used_indices:
                    connected_clauses.append(f"which {phrase(sub_t.predicate, sub_t.object)}")
                    used_indices.add(j)
            # Add the current triple as a location reference
            if t.predicate in ["location", "country", "isPartOf"]:
                connected_clauses.append(f"which is located in {t.object}")
            else:
                connected_clauses.append(f"and {phrase(t.predicate, t.object)}")
            used_indices.add(i)
        else:
            # Check if subject is already mentioned (for relative clauses)
            subject_in_clauses = any(t.subject in clause for clause in main_clauses + connected_clauses)
            if subject_in_clauses:
                connected_clauses.append(f"which {phrase(t.predicate, t.object)}")
                used_indices.add(i)
            else:
                # Try to find if any existing object matches this subject
                for clause in main_clauses + connected_clauses:
                    if t.subject in clause:
                        connected_clauses.append(f"which {phrase(t.predicate, t.object)}")
                        used_indices.add(i)
                        break
                else:
                    # Add as separate clause
                    connected_clauses.append(f"which {phrase(t.predicate, t.object)}")
                    used_indices.add(i)

    clauses = main_clauses + connected_clauses

    # Join clauses into a single fluent sentence
    if len(clauses) == 0:
        sentence = f"{main_subject}."
    elif len(clauses) == 1:
        sentence = f"{main_subject} {clauses[0]}."
    else:
        # Build more natural sentence structure with proper connectors
        if len(clauses) == 2:
            sentence = f"{main_subject} {clauses[0]}, {clauses[1]}."
        else:
            sentence = f"{main_subject} " + ", ".join(clauses[:-1]) + ", and " + clauses[-1] + "."
    return sentence

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
